# Deploying to Posit Connect {#sec-deployment}

This chapter covers deploying your *shinyplanr* application to Posit Connect. We cover prerequisites, server configuration, deployment methods, and troubleshooting.

## Overview

Once you have tested your *shinyplanr* application locally (@sec-setting-up), you can deploy it to Posit Connect so stakeholders can access it via a web browser.

Posit Connect is an enterprise publishing platform that allows you to host Shiny applications, R Markdown documents, Quarto projects, APIs, and more. It provides robust access control, scaling, and monitoring capabilities suitable for production deployments.

## Prerequisites

### 1. Posit Connect Server

Deployment requires access to a Posit Connect server. This may be:

- A server administered by your organisation
- A cloud-hosted Posit Connect instance

Contact your IT department or system administrator if you do not have access. You will need:

- The server URL (e.g., `https://connect.yourorganisation.com/`)
- A user account with publishing permissions

### 2. rsconnect Package

Install the *rsconnect* package:

```r
install.packages("rsconnect")
```

### 3. Server Configuration

Connect your R session to your Posit Connect server. There are two methods:

#### Method A: RStudio IDE

1. In RStudio, go to **Tools** > **Global Options** > **Publishing**
2. Click **Connect...**
3. Select **Posit Connect**
4. Enter your server URL
5. Follow the authentication prompts

[IMAGE: Screenshot of RStudio publishing accounts configuration dialog]

#### Method B: Command Line

```r
rsconnect::connectApiUser(
  account = "your_username",
  server = "connect.yourorganisation.com",
  apiKey = "your_api_key"
)
```

::: {.callout-note}
## API Keys
Obtain your API key from your Posit Connect dashboard under **Settings** > **API Keys**. Keep this key secure and do not commit it to version control.
:::

### 4. Local Testing

Ensure your application runs locally without errors before deploying:

```r
pkgload::load_all()
shinyplanr::run_app()
```

## Pre-Deployment Checklist

Before deploying, verify:

- [ ] `setup-data.R` has been run successfully
- [ ] `setup-app.R` has been run successfully
- [ ] All logos are in `inst/app/www/`
- [ ] The app runs locally without errors
- [ ] All required packages are listed in `DESCRIPTION`
- [ ] `R/sysdata.rda` has been generated

## Deployment Methods

### Method 1: RStudio Deploy Button

The simplest method uses RStudio's built-in deployment:

1. Open `app.R` in RStudio
2. Click the **Publish** button (blue icon, top right of the editor pane)
3. Select your Posit Connect server
4. Choose an app name (or accept the default)
5. Review the files to be included
6. Click **Publish**

[IMAGE: Screenshot of RStudio publish button and deployment dialog]

RStudio will bundle your application and upload it to Posit Connect. Progress is shown in the Deploy tab.

### Method 2: Command Line Deployment

For greater control, use `rsconnect::deployApp()`:

```r
rsconnect::deployApp(
  appDir = ".",
  appName = "MyRegion_shinyplanr",
  server = "connect.yourorganisation.com",
  account = "your_username"
)
```

### Method 3: Custom Deployment Script (Recommended)

For production deployments, create a script that excludes unnecessary files. This reduces deployment size and avoids uploading sensitive development files.

Create or modify `data-raw/ShinyAppsDeploy.R`:

```r
# Deployment script for shinyplanr
# Excludes development files to reduce deployment size

library(tidyverse)
library(rsconnect)

# Enable packrat mode for dependency management
options(rsconnect.packrat = TRUE)

# Get all files in the project
all_files <- list.files(
  path = ".",
  recursive = TRUE,
  all.files = TRUE,
  include.dirs = FALSE
)

# Exclude directories that should not be deployed
files_to_deploy <- all_files %>%
  stringr::str_subset(pattern = "^data-raw/", negate = TRUE) %>%
  stringr::str_subset(pattern = "^.git/", negate = TRUE) %>%
  stringr::str_subset(pattern = "^tests/", negate = TRUE) %>%
  stringr::str_subset(pattern = "^.Rproj.user/", negate = TRUE) %>%
  stringr::str_subset(pattern = "^man/", negate = TRUE) %>%
  stringr::str_subset(pattern = "^inst/manual/", negate = TRUE) %>%
  stringr::str_subset(pattern = "ShinyAppsUpdate", negate = TRUE) %>%
  stringr::str_subset(pattern = "ShinyAppsDeploy", negate = TRUE)

# Deploy the application
rsconnect::deployApp(
  appName = "MyRegion",
  appFiles = files_to_deploy,
  server = "connect.yourorganisation.com",
  account = "your_username"
)
```

Run this script to deploy:

```r
source("data-raw/ShinyAppsDeploy.R")
```

## App Configuration

### Naming Your App

Choose a meaningful, descriptive name:

- Use the region name (e.g., "Fiji_SpatialPlanning", "Kosrae_MSP")
- Avoid spaces and special characters
- Names must be unique within your account

```r
rsconnect::deployApp(
  appName = "Fiji_SpatialPlanning"
)
```

### Access Control

Posit Connect provides flexible access control options:

1. Navigate to your app in the Posit Connect dashboard
2. Go to **Access** settings
3. Configure who can view the application:

| Access Level | Description |
|--------------|-------------|
| **Anyone** | No login required (public access) |
| **All users** | Anyone with a Posit Connect account |
| **Specific users/groups** | Only named users or groups |

For stakeholder consultations, you may want time-limited access. Configure this through the **Scheduled** settings.

[IMAGE: Screenshot of Posit Connect access control settings]

### Runtime Settings

Configure runtime settings for optimal performance:

1. Navigate to your app in the Posit Connect dashboard
2. Go to **Runtime** settings

Key settings include:

| Setting | Description | Recommendation |
|---------|-------------|----------------|
| **Min processes** | Minimum concurrent instances | 0-1 for low traffic |
| **Max processes** | Maximum concurrent instances | 2-5 depending on expected load |
| **Max connections per process** | Concurrent users per instance | 5-20 |
| **Idle timeout** | Time before idle process terminates | 5-15 minutes |
| **Initial timeout** | Max time for app to start | 300+ seconds for large apps |

::: {.callout-important}
## Memory Requirements
*shinyplanr* runs *prioritizr* optimisation in real-time. Larger regions with more features require more memory. If analyses fail or time out, increase the worker memory allocation.
:::

### Environment Variables

If your application requires API keys or other secrets:

1. Go to your app's **Vars** settings in Posit Connect
2. Add environment variables as key-value pairs
3. Access them in R with `Sys.getenv("VARIABLE_NAME")`

Never commit secrets to version control. Use environment variables instead.

## Understanding the Deployment Process

### What Gets Deployed

The deployment includes:

- **R/**: All R code (modules, utilities, functions)
- **inst/app/**: App resources (CSS, images, logos)
- **DESCRIPTION**: Package metadata and dependencies
- **NAMESPACE**: Package exports
- **app.R**: Entry point for the application
- **R/sysdata.rda**: Internal data (created by `setup-app.R`)

### What Gets Excluded

Using the custom deployment script, these are excluded:

- **data-raw/**: Raw data and setup scripts
- **tests/**: Test files
- **man/**: Documentation
- **inst/manual/**: This manual
- **.git/**: Version control
- **.Rproj.user/**: RStudio project files

### Dependency Management

Posit Connect automatically:

1. Reads package dependencies from `DESCRIPTION`
2. Installs required packages on the server
3. Handles GitHub packages listed in `Remotes:`

Ensure all dependencies are listed in `DESCRIPTION`:

```
Imports:
    shiny,
    prioritizr,
    spatialplanr,
    sf,
    terra,
    ...
Remotes:
    github::SpatialPlanning/spatialplanr
```

## Updating an Existing Deployment

To update a deployed application:

### Full Re-deployment

Run the deployment script again. This overwrites the existing app:

```r
source("data-raw/ShinyAppsDeploy.R")
```

### Quick Update

For code-only changes (no data updates), use `forceUpdate`:

```r
rsconnect::deployApp(
  appName = "MyRegion",
  forceUpdate = TRUE
)
```

### Version History

Posit Connect maintains a history of deployments. To revert to a previous version:

1. Navigate to your app in the dashboard
2. Go to **Info** > **Source versions**
3. Select a previous version
4. Click **Activate**

## Monitoring and Logs

### Viewing Logs

Access application logs through the Posit Connect dashboard:

1. Navigate to your application
2. Click the **Logs** tab
3. View real-time logs or filter by time period

Use the log level dropdown to filter by severity (Error, Warning, Info, etc.).

### Common Log Messages

```
Shiny application starting up...
Listening on http://127.0.0.1:XXXX
```
Normal startup - application loaded successfully.

```
Error: object 'xyz' not found
```
A required object is missing. Check that `setup-app.R` ran correctly and `R/sysdata.rda` exists.

```
Error in solve...
```
Solver error. Check that the solver (*highs* or *gurobi*) is available and working.

### Usage Metrics

Posit Connect tracks usage metrics including:

- Number of active users
- Session duration
- Resource utilisation

Access these through **Metrics** in your app's dashboard.

## Troubleshooting

### Deployment Fails

**Error: Unable to determine package source**

Ensure all packages are available from CRAN or listed in `Remotes:` in DESCRIPTION.

```
Remotes:
    github::SpatialPlanning/spatialplanr,
    github::emlab-ucsb/oceandatr
```

**Error: Object not found**

Run `setup-app.R` locally to regenerate `R/sysdata.rda`, then redeploy.

**Error: Application failed to start**

Check the logs for specific errors. Common causes:

- Missing data files
- Package installation failures
- Memory limits exceeded
- Timeout during startup (increase initial timeout)

### App Runs Slowly

- Increase worker memory in Runtime settings
- Reduce the number of planning units
- Consider pre-computing summary statistics

### Analysis Times Out

For long-running optimisations:

- Increase the application timeout in Runtime settings
- Simplify the problem (fewer features, larger planning units)
- Pre-compute results where possible

### Session Disconnects

If users experience frequent disconnections:

- Check the idle timeout setting
- The app includes automatic reconnection handling
- Consider increasing connection timeout settings

## Best Practices

### Version Control

- Use Git to track all changes
- Tag releases before major deployments
- Keep deployment scripts in version control (excluding secrets)

### Documentation

- Document the deployment process for your team
- Maintain a changelog of deployments
- Record server-specific configuration

### Testing Workflow

1. Test locally with `shinyplanr::run_app()`
2. Deploy to a staging environment if available
3. Verify critical functionality after deployment
4. Monitor logs for the first few hours

### Security

- Use environment variables for secrets
- Configure appropriate access controls
- Review user access periodically
- Keep packages updated for security patches

## Summary

Deploying *shinyplanr* to Posit Connect involves:

1. **Prerequisites**: Posit Connect access, *rsconnect* package, local testing
2. **Configuration**: Server connection, app naming
3. **Deployment**: Custom script recommended for clean deployment
4. **Settings**: Access control, runtime configuration
5. **Monitoring**: Check logs, respond to issues
6. **Updates**: Redeploy when code or data changes

Typical deployment workflow:

```r
# 1. Test locally
pkgload::load_all()
shinyplanr::run_app()

# 2. Deploy
source("data-raw/ShinyAppsDeploy.R")

# 3. Monitor
# Check Posit Connect dashboard for logs and metrics
```

## Quick Reference

### Deployment Commands

```r
# Configure server (one-time setup via RStudio IDE or API key)
rsconnect::connectApiUser(account, server, apiKey)

# Simple deployment
rsconnect::deployApp(appName = "MyApp")

# Custom deployment with file selection
rsconnect::deployApp(appName = "MyApp", appFiles = files_to_deploy)

# Force update
rsconnect::deployApp(appName = "MyApp", forceUpdate = TRUE)

# List deployed apps
rsconnect::applications()

# Terminate an app
rsconnect::terminateApp("MyApp")
```

### Useful Links

- [Posit Connect User Guide](https://docs.posit.co/connect/user/)
- [Posit Connect Admin Guide](https://docs.posit.co/connect/admin/)
- [*rsconnect* Package Documentation](https://rstudio.github.io/rsconnect/)
